revision.text:
'''Oyama-jinja''' is a shrine in [[Kanazawa]], [[Ishikawa prefecture]]. It is famous for its Dutch stained-glass window, which dates back several hundred years.
''This article is a [[Wikipedia:Perfect stub article|stub]]. You can help Wikipedia by [[Wikipedia:Find or fix a stub|fixing it]].'' ''The article is originally from [[OpenHistory]].''

revision.comment: added some details to the stub
revision.text:
'''Oyama-jinja''' (尾山神社) is a shrine in [[Kanazawa]], [[Ishikawa prefecture]].
The shrine was established in [[1599]] in Utatsuyama (卯辰山), east of [[Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in [[1873]] and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in [[1875]]. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[Art and architecture of Japan |Japanese]], [[Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[Architectural style|European]] religious [[Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is 25m high including the [[lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[lighthouse]]. The gate was designed a Important Cultural Asset on August 29, [[1950]].

This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[Art and architecture of Japan |Japanese]], [[Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[Architectural style|European]] religious [[Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is 25m high including the [[lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[lighthouse]]. The gate was designed a Important Cultural Asset on August 29, [[1950]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اوياما]][[commons:Category:Oyama-jinja]][[de:Oyama-Schrein (Kanazawa)]][[en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)]][[eu:Oyama santutegia]][[fr:Oyama-jinja (Ishikawa)]][[it:Santuario di Oyama]][[ja:尾山神社]][[zh:尾山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q998709]] Wikidata name: Oyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Oyama Shrine (disambiguation){{!}}Oyama Shrine}}{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} | image = File:Ishikawa Kanazawa Oyama03.jpg | caption = Main gate | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.566388888889|N|136.65527777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Maeda Matsu|qid=Q836600}}; {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|qid=Q1196817}} | founded_by ={{ill|Maeda Naonobu|qid=Q11397664}} | established = 1599 | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|qid=Q191130}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]] (the first lord of [[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]]), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[:en:Shinto architecture|European]] religious [[:en:Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the [[:en:Lightning rod|Lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[:en:Netherlands|Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[:en:en:Lighthouse|en:Lighthouse]]. The gate was designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Asset]] on August 29, 1950.

{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]] (the first lord of [[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]]), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[:en:Shinto architecture|European]] religious [[:en:Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the [[:en:Lightning rod|Lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[:en:Netherlands|Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[:en:en:Lighthouse|en:Lighthouse]]. The gate was designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Asset]] on August 29, 1950.

{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]] (the first lord of [[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]]), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[:en:Shinto architecture|European]] religious [[:en:Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the [[:en:Lightning rod|Lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[:en:Netherlands|Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[:en:en:Lighthouse|en:Lighthouse]]. The gate was designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Asset]] on August 29, 1950.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اوياما]][[sn:commons:Category:Oyama-jinja]][[de:Oyama-Schrein (Kanazawa)]][[en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)]][[eu:Oyama santutegia]][[fr:Oyama-jinja (Ishikawa)]][[it:Santuario di Oyama]][[ja:尾山神社]][[zh:尾山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q998709]] Wikidata name: Oyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Oyama Shrine (disambiguation){{!}}Oyama Shrine}}{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} | image = File:Ishikawa Kanazawa Oyama03.jpg | caption = Main gate | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.566388888889|N|136.65527777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Maeda Matsu|WD=Q836600}}; {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817}} | founded_by ={{ill|Maeda Naonobu|WD=Q11397664}} | established = 1599 | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]] (the first lord of [[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]]), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[:en:Shinto architecture|European]] religious [[:en:Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the [[:en:Lightning rod|Lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[:en:Netherlands|Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[:en:en:Lighthouse|en:Lighthouse]]. The gate was designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Asset]] on August 29, 1950.

{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]] (the first lord of [[:en:Kaga Domain|Kaga Domain]]), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa]]. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and [[:en:Shinto architecture|European]] religious [[:en:Architecture|architectural]] elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the [[:en:Lightning rod|Lightning rod]]. The third floor is particular famous for its [[:en:Netherlands|Dutch]] stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a [[:en:en:Lighthouse|en:Lighthouse]]. The gate was designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Asset]] on August 29, 1950.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اوياما]][[co:commons:Category:Oyama-jinja]][[de:Oyama-Schrein (Kanazawa)]][[en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)]][[eu:Oyama santutegia]][[fr:Oyama-jinja (Ishikawa)]][[it:Santuario di Oyama]][[ja:尾山神社]][[zh:尾山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اوياما]][[co:commons:Category:Oyama-jinja]][[de:Oyama-Schrein (Kanazawa)]][[en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)]][[eu:Oyama santutegia]][[fr:Oyama-jinja (Ishikawa)]][[it:Santuario di Oyama]][[ja:尾山神社]][[zh:尾山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ضريح اوياما]][[sn:commons:Category:Oyama-jinja]][[de:Oyama-Schrein (Kanazawa)]][[en:Oyama Shrine (Ishikawa)]][[eu:Oyama santutegia]][[fr:Oyama-jinja (Ishikawa)]][[it:Santuario di Oyama]][[ja:尾山神社]][[zh:尾山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q998709]] Wikidata name: Oyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Other uses|Oyama Shrine (disambiguation){{!}}Oyama Shrine}}{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} | image = File:Ishikawa Kanazawa Oyama03.jpg | caption = Main gate | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.566388888889|N|136.65527777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Maeda Matsu|WD=Q836600}}; {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817}} | founded_by ={{ill|Maeda Naonobu|WD=Q11397664}} | established = 1599 | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}} (the first lord of {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain}}), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=European}} religious {{ill|Architecture|en|Architecture|WD=Q12271|lt=architectural}} elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the {{ill|Lightning rod|en|Lightning rod|WD=Q7973|lt=Lightning rod}}. The third floor is particular famous for its {{ill|Netherlands|en|Netherlands|WD=Q55|lt=Dutch}} stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a {{ill|en:Lighthouse|en|en:Lighthouse|WD=Q39715|lt=en:Lighthouse}}. The gate was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Asset}} on August 29, 1950.

{{Other uses|Oyama Shrine (disambiguation){{!}}Oyama Shrine}}{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} | image = File:Ishikawa Kanazawa Oyama03.jpg | caption = Main gate | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.566388888889|N|136.65527777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Maeda Matsu|WD=Q836600}}; {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817}} | founded_by ={{ill|Maeda Naonobu|WD=Q11397664}} | established = 1599 | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}} (the first lord of {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain}}), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa}}. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=European}} religious {{ill|Architecture|en|Architecture|WD=Q12271|lt=architectural}} elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the {{ill|Lightning rod|en|Lightning rod|WD=Q7973|lt=Lightning rod}}. The third floor is particular famous for its {{ill|Netherlands|en|Netherlands|WD=Q55|lt=Dutch}} stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a {{ill|en:Lighthouse|en|en:Lighthouse|WD=Q39715|lt=en:Lighthouse}}. The gate was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Asset}} on August 29, 1950.

{{Other uses|Oyama Shrine (disambiguation){{!}}Oyama Shrine}}{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} | image = File:Ishikawa Kanazawa Oyama03.jpg | caption = Main gate | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.566388888889|N|136.65527777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Maeda Matsu|WD=Q836600|1=Maeda Matsu}}; {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|1=Maeda Toshiie}} | founded_by ={{ill|Maeda Naonobu|WD=Q11397664|1=Maeda Naonobu}} | established = 1599 | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Oyama Shrine'''|尾山神社|Oyama-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-06 |title=A Summer Stroll Through Green Kanazawa |url=https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-video/ct171000015/a-summer-stroll-through-green-kanazawa.html |access-date=2022-11-04 |website=nippon.com |language=en}}</ref>
The shrine was established in 1599, dedicated to {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie|1=Maeda Toshiie}} (the first lord of {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|WD=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain|1=Kaga Domain}}), in [[Mount Utatsu|Utatsu-yama]] (卯辰山), east of {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa|1=Kanazawa}}. It was moved to its present location in 1873 and renamed to '''Oyama-jinja'''. The main gate was constructed in 1875. This gate is a peculiar mix of traditional [[:en:Art and architecture of Japan|Japanese]], [[:en:Culture of China|Chinese]], and {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=European|1=Shinto architecture}} religious {{ill|Architecture|en|Architecture|WD=Q12271|lt=architectural|1=Architecture}} elements. The gate is {{convert|25|m}} high including the {{ill|Lightning rod|en|Lightning rod|WD=Q7973|lt=Lightning rod|1=Lightning rod}}. The third floor is particular famous for its {{ill|Netherlands|en|Netherlands|WD=Q55|lt=Dutch|1=Netherlands}} stained-glass windows. It is said that the third floor was also used as a {{ill|en:Lighthouse|en|en:Lighthouse|WD=Q39715|lt=en:Lighthouse|1=Lighthouse}}. The gate was designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Asset|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on August 29, 1950.
